heading: Actions by the Secretary of State in response to patterns of noncompliance in cases of international child abductions
label: 9122 Actions by the Secretary of State in response to patterns of noncompliance in cases of international child abductions

children.heading: Response to a pattern of noncompliance
children.label: a Response to a pattern of noncompliance

children.text: to oppose institutional or other systemic failures of foreign governments to fulfill their obligations pursuant to the Hague Abduction Convention or bilateral procedures, as applicable, to resolve abduction and access cases;
to promote reciprocity pursuant to, and in compliance with, the Hague Abduction Convention or bilateral procedures, as appropriate; and
to directly engage with senior foreign government officials to most effectively address patterns of noncompliance.

children.heading: Determination of countries with patterns of noncompliance in cases of international child abduction
children.label: b Determination of countries with patterns of noncompliance in cases of international child abduction
A
children.text: review the status of abduction and access cases in each foreign country in order to determine whether the government of such country has engaged in a pattern of noncompliance during the preceding 12 months; and

children.label: B
children.text: report such determination pursuant to .section 9111(f) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to appropriately target actions in response to such noncompliance; and

children.label: B
children.text: to engage with senior foreign government officials to effectively address such noncompliance.

children.heading: Actions by the Secretary of State with respect to a country with a pattern of noncompliance
children.label: c Actions by the Secretary of State with respect to a country with a pattern of noncompliance
A
children.text: take 1 or more of the actions described in subsection (d);

children.label: B
children.text: direct the Chief of Mission in that country to directly address the systemic problems that led to such determination; and

children.label: C
children.text: inform senior officials in the foreign government of the potential repercussions related to such designation.

children.heading: Authority for delay of actions by the Secretary of State
children.label: 2 Authority for delay of actions by the Secretary of State
A
children.text: for a continuation of negotiations that have been commenced with the government of a country described in paragraph (1) in order to bring about a cessation of the pattern of noncompliance by such country;

children.label: B
children.text: for a review of corrective action taken by a country after the designation of such country as being engaged in a pattern of noncompliance under subsection (b)(1)(A); or

children.label: C
children.text: in anticipation that corrective action will be taken by such country during such 90-day period.

children.heading: Exception for additional action by the Secretary of State
children.label: 3 Exception for additional action by the Secretary of State
A
children.text: has taken action pursuant to paragraph (5), (6), or (7) of subsection (d) with respect to such country in the preceding year and such action continues to be in effect;

children.label: B
children.text: exercises the waiver under and briefs the appropriate congressional committees; orsection 9124 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: indicates that such country is subject to multiple, broad-based sanctions; and

children.label: ii
children.text: describes how such sanctions satisfy the requirements under this subsection.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 4 Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the submission of the Annual Report, the Secretary shall submit a report to Congress on the specific actions taken against countries determined to have been engaged in a pattern of noncompliance under this section.

children.heading: Description of actions by the Secretary of State in Hague Abduction Convention countries
children.label: d Description of actions by the Secretary of State in Hague Abduction Convention countries

children.text: a demarche;
an official public statement detailing unresolved cases;
a public condemnation;
a delay or cancellation of 1 or more bilateral working, official, or state visits;
the withdrawal, limitation, or suspension of United States development assistance in accordance with section 116 of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ();22 U.S.C. 2151n
the withdrawal, limitation, or suspension of United States security assistance in accordance with section 502B of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ();22 U.S.C. 2304

the withdrawal, limitation, or suspension of assistance to the central government of a country pursuant to chapter 4 of part II of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.; relating to the Economic Support Fund); and22 U.S.C. 2346
a formal request to the foreign country concerned to extradite an individual who is engaged in abduction and who has been formally accused of, charged with, or convicted of an extraditable offense.

children.label: A
children.text: is commensurate in effect to the action substituted; and

children.label: B
children.text: would substantially further the purposes of this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: describes such action;

children.label: B
children.text: explains the reasons for taking such action; and

children.label: i
children.text: is commensurate with the action substituted; and

children.label: ii
children.text: substantially furthers the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Resolution
children.label: f Resolution
children.text: The Secretary of State shall seek to take all appropriate actions authorized by law to resolve the unresolved case or to obtain the cessation of such pattern of noncompliance, as applicable.

children.heading: Humanitarian exception
children.label: g Humanitarian exception
children.text: Any action taken pursuant to subsection (d) or (e) may not prohibit or restrict the provision of medicine, medical equipment or supplies, food, or other life-saving humanitarian assistance.

children.heading: Study of international parental child abduction
children.label: h Study of international parental child abduction

children.heading: Study required
children.label: 1 Study required
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary of State, subject to the availability of funds, shall seek to enter into an agreement with an appropriate university, research institution, or nongovernmental organization to study and publish a report on the impact to abducted children and left-behind parents as a result of international parental child abduction.December 23, 2024

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: The Secretary of State shall consult with the appropriate congressional committees on the goals of the study and report required under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated $1,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2025 and 2026 to carry out the study required under paragraph (1).
